I think, yes, we definitely have the feature, but the way we are positioning, so I'll tell you the pushback that we usually get from brands, like, it's really hard for them to implement live stream because it is very resource intensive, and the name itself brings up this idea that, hey, you need to have, like, a few folks who are actually presenting a product, or selling a product or explaining that, and it is, it requires a lot of production, resources, scripts, and all of that, and it's just, like, a one-time event.
We are trying to do away or move away with the idea of, like, this is not, like, a one-time thing, but something that you do once, and then you can keep replaying it again and again on your website.
So, a better way to position that would be 24-7 live stream.

Because live stream, we don't want people to think, okay, we do like a 30 minute live stream on a one hour live stream, we get some traffic, let's say 20, 30k people on the website engaging with us, we get some sales and that's it.

Because then the ROI looks really bad from a brand perspective because they're spending like thousands of dollars planning and executing an event, whereas if we position it in a way saying that, okay, this is like, okay, you plan a live stream, execute it, but you can use these videos on your website 24-7 across different pages to engage with your audience and then suddenly the ROI shoots up.
And we have seen traditionally, like if a brand is actually re-streaming this content 24-7, then the engagement is far, far greater than just looking at, you know, a traditional live stream.
So that was the only idea.
I am not.
